title: Labor-Market Research, Role Families, and Data Limits · Hiring Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A hiring panel compares near-miss decisions for labor-market research, role families, and data limits and accepts only role-relevant, truthful, accessible, privacy-preserving, and independently defensible evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for dated labor-market evidence brief. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish occupations, job titles, functions, industries, products, seniority, and employment arrangements when mapping possible work.
Record source owner, geography, population, collection period, update date, method, and missing groups before using labor data.
Interpret posting-frequency signals as bounded demand evidence rather than required-skill, hiring-probability, or future-certainty claims.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Role Hypothesis
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A viral salary post combines national medians, remote listings, senior titles, old data, and one technology trend into a guaranteed entry-level forecast. The quiz variation 4 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Select a small, testable role-family hypothesis from personal constraints, evidence adjacency, real tasks, and market signals.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Role Hypothesis.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for select a small, testable role-family hypothesis from personal constraints, evidence adjacency, real tasks, and market signals. For Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Role Hypothesis, keep Role Hypothesis connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A viral salary post combines national medians, remote listings, senior titles, old data, and one technology trend into a guaranteed entry-level forecast. The quiz variation 4 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Select a small, testable role-family hypothesis from personal constraints, evidence adjacency, real tasks, and market signals.

content.title: Fault-isolation decision
content.text: Select evidence that identifies a cause, not a patch that only hides the Role Hypothesis symptom.

stimulus.title: Role Hypothesis failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Keeping every possible role open always increases opportunity.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Select a small, testable role-family hypothesis from personal constraints, evidence adjacency, real tasks, and market signals.

options.text: Run the boundary case, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.
Accept the result because visible output is the highest authority. That claim fails the evidence boundary for Role Hypothesis.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
